rulings.Case Title: EICON INC VS WORLDPOINT INTERACTIVE INC
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Re-Notice) Ofhearing Adte For X-Complaints' Motion For Sanctions Filed Jul-28-09
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 18, 2010, line 1, CROSS COMPLAINANTS SNYDER TRUST ENTERPRISES, JOHN POPPIN, 706 SANSOME PROPERTIES, MARGARET POPPIN (Re-Notice) of hearing for Cross-Complainants' Motion for Sanctions Filed Jul-28-09. Is denied. The renotice of the sanctions motion is improper because Snyder has not exercised diligence as required by CCP section 128.7(c), and 16 court day notice was not provided. The "objection" is not a proper way to obtain sanctions and is also untimely under CCP section 1005. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ISA MUHAWIEH VS. MAHER MUHAWIEH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion To Dismiss For Failure To Prosecute
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 18, 2010, line 2, DEFENDANT RAMI BALAT Motion to Dismiss for Failure to Prosecute. Is denied. Insufficient evidence presented that plaintiff was dilatory in prosecuting this action. Plaintiff actively responded to the OSC and filed a motion to vacate the arbitration order, both within the past year. Plaintiff also settled with the other defendant in mid-2008. Defendant fails to demonstrate any real prejudice. Public policy supports hearing this case on the merits. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JUDY WILLIAMS VS. BLUE SHIELD OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 18, 2010, line 11, DEFENDANT CALIFORNIA PHYSICIAN'S SERVICE DBA BLUE SHIELD AMENDED DEMURRER TO SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with 10 days leave to amend. Plaintiffs must clearly allege the terms of the contract, breach, and their status as third party beneficiaries to support the first and second causes of action. As to the third cause of action, plaintiff must specify the particular conduct they seek to enjoin defendant from. As to the fourth cause of action, plaintiffs must either allege violations of statutory or regulatory provisions, plead fraud with specificity, or plead a duty that would support a claim for unfair business practices. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: EMMANUELLE KHOII VS. LILYA VAISMAN DR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 18, 2010, line 3, PLAINTIFF EMMANUELLE KHOII MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD. Granted. No opposition filed. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: VICTORIA L PETERSON VS. LUIS ADAME et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 18, 2010, line 4, DEFENDANT LUIS ADAME MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. Motion to strike second cause of action and punitive damages claim granted with 10 days leave to amend. The allegations do not support an intentional tort. Insufficient facts alleged to demonstrate oppression, fraud, or malice required to support punitive damages claim. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: PANCHO VILLA LICENSING LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. AP DELI AND FOODS INC A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion & Motion To Tax Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 18, 2010, line 5, DEFENDANTS TULIO SILVA, VIRGINIA SILVA, AP DELI & FOODS, INC. Motion to Tax Costs. Is granted. No opposition filed. Defendants awarded reasonable attorney fees ($500) and costs ($40) for bringing this motion. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ZAKARIAH LAFRENIERE VS. THE BOARD OF TRUSTEE OF THE CALIFORNIA STATE
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice And Motion To Vacate Court Order Of 4/23/10 Errantly Denying Motion For Summary Judgment As "Moot" And For Reinstatement Of Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 18, 2010, line 6, PLAINTIFF ZAKARIAH LAFRENIERE, Motion to Vacate Court Order Of 4/23/10 Errantly Denying Motion for Summary Judgment as "Moot" and for Reinstatement of Motion for Summary Judgment. Motion denied. On September 18, 2009 the Court sustained defendant's unopposed demurrer with leave to amend for the reasons set forth in the October 13, 2009 order sustaining Demurrer without leave to amend and dismissing the matter with prejudice. No amended complaint was or could be filed and summary judgment was filed in a dismissed case. No good cause demonstrated to vacate the 4/23/2010 order or to reinstate the motion for summary judgment. Case was dismissed more than six months ago. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN RYNNE VS. RICHARD M HOHL et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: (Amended) Notice Of Motion And Motion For Sanctions Pursuant To Ccp 128.7
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 18, 2010, line 7, CROSS DEFENDANTS RICHARD HOHL, RMH PARTNERSHIP, LLC (Amended) Notice of Motion and Motion for Sanctions Pursuant To CCP 128.7. Motion for sanctions is granted. Cross-defendants awarded reasonable attorneys fees and costs in the amount of $4,150 from cross-complainant John O'Rourke. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: JOHN HICKS et al VS. FRANK YUEN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc And Motion For Leave To File Cross Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 18, 2010, line 9, DEFENDANTS FRANK YUEN, and, MAE YUEN Motion for Leave to File Cross Complaint. Is granted. CCP section 426.50 is to be liberally construed to avoid forfeiture of causes of action. The Yuens must file their cross-complaint within 10 days. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A. VS. TIMOTHY J KODANI et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday, June 18, 2010, line 8, DEFENDANT TIMOTHY KODANI DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Is overruled, five days leave to answer. Plaintiff properly pleads that it is the owner of 2 Fallon Place Unit #9. A factual issue remains whether plaintiff has properly pleaded the address. =(302/CWW)
